# John Whitton

> John Whitton's personal website

## About John

Hello, I'm John Whitton.

I am an engineer, architect, and leader focused on building complex systems across Trading Systems, Financial Infrastructure, DeFi Protocol Engineering, Distributed Systems, and AI Engineering.

Over the past twenty-plus years I have worked across startups, enterprise organizations, blockchain networks, financial platforms, and emerging technologies. My career has consistently focused on understanding complex systems, designing practical solutions, and helping teams deliver them successfully.

### Engineer

I enjoy building systems.

My recent work includes trading infrastructure, liquidity mapping, route evaluation, stablecoin infrastructure, protocol engineering, distributed systems, and developer platforms. I remain a hands-on engineer and continue to spend significant time designing, building, testing, and operating production systems.

I am particularly interested in systems that combine performance, scalability, and real-world business outcomes.

### Architect

Architecture has been a constant theme throughout my career.

My earlier work focused on enterprise integration, service-oriented architectures, and large-scale business platforms. Over time that evolved into blockchain infrastructure, protocol design, financial infrastructure, and trading systems.

Across each generation of technology, the underlying challenge has remained the same: understanding how complex systems interact and designing solutions that can evolve over time.

### Leader

I enjoy helping teams succeed.

Throughout my career I have worked as a founder, CTO, engineering manager, architect, and technical leader. I have built and scaled teams, hired engineers, mentored individuals, and led cross-functional initiatives across startups and enterprise organizations.

The most rewarding part of leadership is helping talented people grow while delivering meaningful outcomes together.

### Career Evolution

My career has followed a natural progression:

```text
Enterprise Systems
-> Architecture Leadership
-> Blockchain Infrastructure
-> Financial Infrastructure
-> Trading Systems
-> AI Engineering
```

Each stage built upon the previous one.

Enterprise systems provided a foundation in scale and integration. Blockchain introduced distributed networks and protocol design. Financial infrastructure expanded into payments and settlement systems. Trading systems brought performance, execution, routing, and market infrastructure. AI engineering is now creating entirely new ways to design, build, and operate software systems.

### Current Focus

Today I am most interested in work at the intersection of:

* Trading Systems
* Financial Infrastructure
* DeFi Protocol Engineering
* Distributed Systems
* AI Engineering

I continue to explore these domains through engineering, architecture, research, mentoring, advisory work, and product development.


## Example

This is an example page.


## Get started

Hello world!


## Resume

Choose the resume that best matches the role. Each version presents the same career through a different technical or leadership lens while highlighting the most relevant experience for that audience.

**Resume Downloads:** <a className="vocs_Anchor" href="/images/john_em.pdf">Leadership</a> · <a className="vocs_Anchor" href="/images/john.pdf">Trading Infrastructure</a> · <a className="vocs_Anchor" href="/images/john_defi.pdf">DeFi Protocol Engineering</a> · <a className="vocs_Anchor" href="/images/john_payments.pdf">Financial Infrastructure</a> · <a className="vocs_Anchor" href="/images/john_rust.pdf">Platform & Distributed Systems</a>

### Which resume should I read?

| Resume                             | Best suited for                                                                                 |
| ---------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Leadership**                     | Engineering Manager, Director, Head of Engineering, Staff+ leadership roles                     |
| **Trading Infrastructure**         | Trading systems, execution infrastructure, MEV, market infrastructure, quantitative engineering |
| **DeFi Protocol Engineering**      | DeFi protocols, Solidity, smart contracts, intents, settlement systems                          |
| **Financial Infrastructure**       | Stablecoins, payments, cross-chain settlement, banking infrastructure, fintech                  |
| **Platform & Distributed Systems** | Rust, backend infrastructure, distributed systems, platform engineering                         |

***

## Executive Summary

**Engineer. Architect. Leader.**

Engineering leader, architect, founder, and hands-on systems builder specializing in trading infrastructure, financial infrastructure, DeFi protocol engineering, distributed systems, and AI-assisted engineering.

Over the past 20+ years I have designed, built, led, and delivered production systems spanning enterprise integration, payments, stablecoin infrastructure, blockchain networks, execution platforms, and financial systems. Recent work has focused on modern Rust trading infrastructure, protocol engineering, distributed systems, and technical architecture.

Recent scope and outcomes:

* Built Rust-based trading infrastructure evaluating 1,100+ routes/sec
* Built route graphs spanning 17,000+ assets, 8,000+ liquidity pools, and 2.3M+ routes
* Led stablecoin payment infrastructure and cross-chain settlement initiatives
* Supported distributed blockchain infrastructure spanning 300+ nodes and 600+ validators
* Scaled engineering organizations from early-stage startups through enterprise delivery environments

For a complete overview of engineering projects, architecture, technical leadership, and system design, view the [Portfolio Presentation](https://portfolio.johnwhitton.com).

***

## Career Snapshot

### Engineering Leadership

* Scaled Harmony engineering organization from 6 to 20 engineers
* Led cross-functional teams of up to 10 engineers, product, and research staff
* Managed annual planning portfolio exceeding $3M across 100+ initiatives
* Founded and scaled Global Enterprise Solutions to approximately $800k annual revenue

### Trading Infrastructure

* Built Rust-based execution infrastructure evaluating 1,100+ routes/sec
* Built route graphs spanning 17,000+ assets, 8,000+ liquidity pools, and 2.3M+ routes
* Designed liquidity mapping, route evaluation, simulation, pricing, execution, and settlement systems
* Developed historical replay and strategy analytics platforms

### Financial Infrastructure

* Led stablecoin payment infrastructure and programmable money initiatives at Eco
* Built cross-chain routing and settlement infrastructure
* Delivered enterprise financial and loyalty infrastructure for IBM, Deloitte, Emirates Airlines, and CIBC

### Distributed Systems

* Built a Rust-based Polkadot parachain and distributed application platform
* Supported Harmony Mainnet launch, staking infrastructure, APIs, SDKs, and 50+ production upgrades
* Supported infrastructure spanning 300+ nodes and 600+ validators
* Built large-scale enterprise integration, messaging, and API platforms

### Protocol Engineering

* Designed and implemented solver, liquidity, and route evaluation systems
* Built intent-based execution systems and protocol infrastructure
* Designed governance, tokenomics, validator, and protocol architecture for EAVE

### AI Engineering

* Applied AI-assisted development workflows to engineering delivery, documentation, and architecture
* Used AI to accelerate engineering, refactoring, technical research, and system design
* Developed engineering knowledge systems, documentation, and architecture case studies

***

## Professional Experience

### Principal Engineer / Founder

#### Independent Research & Development (2018–Present)

Research, protocol development, fundraising, and consulting across trading infrastructure, financial infrastructure, DeFi protocol engineering, distributed systems, and AI-assisted engineering.

Primary initiatives:

* Salus
* Seamless
* EAVE
* Jincubator research and prototyping

### Engineering Manager / Senior Protocol Engineer

#### Eco (2023–2025)

* Led cross-functional engineering team of 8–10 engineers
* Built stablecoin infrastructure and programmable money systems
* Delivered cross-chain routing and settlement infrastructure
* Managed hiring, onboarding, performance reviews, and delivery execution

### Ecosystem Architect

#### Harmony (2019–2020)

* Scaled engineering organization from 6 to 20 engineers
* Delivered mainnet launch, staking infrastructure, APIs, and SDKs
* Coordinated 50+ production upgrades
* Supported 300+ nodes and 600+ validators
* Supported developer ecosystem growth and validator onboarding

### Chief Technology Officer

#### Loyyal (2016–2018)

* Led engineering teams across the United States and Africa
* Built enterprise blockchain and loyalty infrastructure
* Delivered solutions for IBM, Deloitte, Emirates Airlines, and CIBC
* Managed architecture, engineering delivery, and customer implementation

### Enterprise Architect / Founder / Engineering Manager

#### EFI, Autodesk, Global Enterprise Solutions (1986–2016)

* Managed $3M annual planning portfolio
* Coordinated delivery across 100+ initiatives and 50+ staff
* Built large-scale SOA and enterprise integration platforms
* Founded and scaled Global Enterprise Solutions to approximately $800k annual revenue

***

## Education

Bachelor of Computer Science

University of Queensland


#### [Andy](https://www.linkedin.com/in/andy-wu-4329664/)

John is one of the most professional engineers I’ve ever worked with. He takes the ownership of many OPS works,
and documents them down with meticulous details. Also, he is a fast learner, and eager to pick up new skills. He also has very good project management skills, and I believe he could be a very good leader either in biz or eng departments.


#### [Ganesha](https://www.linkedin.com/in/gupadhyaya/)

John is dedicated, hard-working, great work ethics, and brings lot of positive energy. no doubt that he is a great manager with good people skills. john led the whole hiring process and single handedly managed it well, without much chaos. no joke how much time and energy it takes. great job. while i believe it was not the best use of john's time, he embraced whatever this staking launch demanded from him. great work towards managing the release (deployment, documentation, etc) and taking some load of andy and daniel. after the staking release, john could refocus and quickly get back to speed on the ecosystem/tooling/partner tasks/goals for q4 and expedite some of the deliverables.


import { ZoomImage } from "../../public/components/ZoomImage";

## Testimonials & References

<ZoomImage src="/images/HarmonyTeam.jpg" alt="Harmony team" title="Harmony Team" />

I have been fortunate to work with exceptional engineers, architects, researchers, founders, and leaders throughout my career.

The comments below come from colleagues, managers, team members, and collaborators across startups, enterprise organizations, and blockchain ecosystems.

Although the projects have varied, the feedback tends to repeat a consistent set of themes: leadership, mentorship, systems thinking, execution, and helping teams succeed.

### Leadership Themes

> "A natural leader."

> "John is one of the most professional engineers I’ve ever worked with."

> "He could be a very good leader either in biz or eng departments."

> "John has the rare gift of being able to understand complex business problems and map them to a technical solution."

> "John is amazing at explaining and delegating."

> "Thank you for listening and being a great mentor."

> "John has always been so selfless and caring."

> "John has the biggest heart."

> "You always took care of team mates."

> "You made me feel instantly welcome and gave me a sense of ownership."

> "John does a good job of recruiting and onboarding new interns."

> "John focused a lot on knowledge transfer."

> "He can troubleshoot tons of deployment issues from the core protocol all the way up to the application layer."

> "His excellent communication skills and deep technical knowledge were key factors in the successful rollout of the program."

### Harmony Team Feedback

#### [Janet](https://www.linkedin.com/in/janet-liang-754090135/)

John is always willing to work on whatever the team needs even though you don't necessarily want to.
thank you for listening and being a great mentor.


#### [Li](https://www.linkedin.com/in/lijiang2087/)

I want to thank John for having a detailed process and documentation of the OSTN network upgrades and resets and communicating to me and community.


#### [Matt](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mattlockyer/)

John is amazing at explaining and delegating. I’m always impressed with his knowledge base. I think more efforts could be made to capture his knowledge in “explainers”, that would go a long way to supporting the community. John can also run anything, like up and running in a matter of minutes. This means he can troubleshoot tons of deployment issues from the core protocol all the way up to the application layer


#### [Nick](https://www.linkedin.com/in/nickwh8te/)

John is always willing to jump on whatever needs to get done. he jumped SDK to hiring and then back into network ops. I think that John does a good job of recruiting and onboarding new interns and giving them appropriate difficulty tasks as they ramp up. I'm grateful that he stepped in to cover miami devcon for me at the last minute. team player


#### [Olivier](https://www.linkedin.com/in/olivierveyrac/)

John and I worked together at Autodesk during the implementation of the subscription program, John was a lead SAP developer working on the program. I frequently interacted with him as we rolled out the program. John has the rare gift of being able to understand complex business problems and map them to a technical solution. His drive and passion for the project, combined with his excellent communication skills and deep technical knowledge, were key factors in the successful rollout of the program. We've since become close friends. I'd recommend anyone looking for a technical lead with business acumen to consider John.


#### [Yishuang](https://www.linkedin.com/in/chenyishuang/)

John is a really good team player, among his good qualities, I admire three most.

1. Helpful and warm-hearted whenever you ask for help, John will give you the best help.
2. Avid learner, John likes to learn new things and will actively take new tasks. I see John actively in ops recently.
3. Responsible, longer view, John focused a lot on knowledge transfer. He wants to make sure the knowledge can be shared with the whole team and future new employees, so he contributed a lot on internal wiki gitbooks, which I think it’s a good point.
